name: Fehu

old_icelandic.original: Fé er frænda róg ok flæðar viti ok grafseiðs gata.
old_icelandic.translation: Wealth is source of discord among kin and fire of the sea and path of the serpent.

old_norwegian.original: Fé vældr frænda róge; føðesk ulfr í skóge.
old_norwegian.translation: Wealth causes friction among kinsmen; the wolf thrives in the woods.

anglo_saxon.original: Feoh byþ fruma fira gehwylcum; sceal ðeah manna gehwylc miclun hyt dælan...
anglo_saxon.translation: Wealth is a comfort to all; yet must every man bestow it freely if he wish to gain honor.

practical_application: Managing mobile capital, establishing reciprocity (Gifting Cycle), and social status.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Carve ᚠ into wood while naming the resource to be shared.
Recite the Anglo-Saxon stanza to affirm the intent of generosity.

name: Uruz

old_icelandic.original: Úr er skýja grátr ok skára þverrir ok hirðis hatr.
old_icelandic.translation: Rain is lamentation of the clouds and destroyer of the hay-harvest.

old_norwegian.original: Úr er af illu jarne; opt løypr ræinn á hjarne.
old_norwegian.translation: Dross comes from bad iron; the reindeer often races over the frozen snow.

anglo_saxon.original: Ur byþ anmod ond oferhyrned, felafrecne deor, feohteþ mid hornum...
anglo_saxon.translation: The aurochs is proud and has great horns; it is a very savage beast.

practical_application: Endurance, raw physical strength, and the transition from wild to worked.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Focus on the Aurochs' horns during the carving process.

name: Thurisaz

old_icelandic.original: Þurs er kvenna kvöl ok kletta búi ok varðrúnar verr.
old_icelandic.translation: Giant is torture of women and cliff-dweller and husband of a giantess.

old_norwegian.original: Þurs vældr kvinna kvillu; kátr værðr fár af illu.
old_norwegian.translation: Giant causes anguish to women; misfortune makes few men cheerful.

anglo_saxon.original: Ðorn byþ ðearle scearp; ðegna gehwylcum anfeng ys yfyl...
anglo_saxon.translation: The thorn is exceedingly sharp; an evil thing for any knight to touch.

practical_application: Active defense, breaking through chaotic barriers, and warding.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Invoke the 'Thorn-Wall' to protect the perimeter of the ritual space.

name: Ansuz

old_icelandic.original: Óss er aldingautr ok ásgarðs jöfurr, ok valhallar vísi.
old_icelandic.translation: God is the aged Gautr and prince of Asgard and lord of Valhalla.

anglo_saxon.original: Os byþ ordfruma ælere spræce, wisdomes wraþu ond witena frofur...
anglo_saxon.translation: The mouth is the source of all language, the mainstay of wisdom and comfort of wise men.

practical_application: Wisdom, communication, and anchoring an oath.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Speak the rune name aloud to 'wake' the breath of inspiration.

name: Raidho

old_icelandic.original: Reið er sitjandi sæla ok snörp ferð ok jórs erfiði.
old_icelandic.translation: Riding is a joy for the sitter and a swift journey and labor for the horse.

anglo_saxon.original: Rad byþ on recede rinca gehwylcum sefte ond swiðhwat...
anglo_saxon.translation: Riding seems easy to every warrior while he is indoors and courageous on the road.

practical_application: Rhythmic action and proper timing on a journey.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Establish a rhythmic breath or drumbeat before carving.

name: Kenaz

old_norwegian.original: Kaun er barna böl; böl gørver mann fölan.
old_norwegian.translation: Ulcer is fatal to children; sickness makes a man pale.

anglo_saxon.original: Cen byþ cwicera gehwam, cuþ on fyre blac ond beorhtlic...
anglo_saxon.translation: The torch is known to every living man by its pale, bright flame.

practical_application: Controlled fire vs. the burning of decay; used for illumination.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Light a candle to represent the 'Torch' before carving.

name: Gebo

anglo_saxon.original: Gifu gumena byþ gleng ond herenys, wraþu ond wyrþscipe...
anglo_saxon.translation: Generosity is a decoration and praise to all men.

practical_application: Reciprocity and social contracts.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Place an offering in the bowl to establish the reciprocal bond.

name: Wunjo

anglo_saxon.original: Wenne bruceþ, þe can weana lyt, sares ond sorge...
anglo_saxon.translation: He enjoys joy who knows few troubles, pains, or sorrows.

practical_application: Harmonious group energy and internal stability.

ceremony_roadmap.action: State a positive outcome or shared success.

name: Hagalaz

old_norwegian.original: Hagall er kaldastr korna; Kristr skóp hæiminn gamla.
old_norwegian.translation: Hail is the coldest of grain; Christ created the world of old.

anglo_saxon.original: Hægl byþ hwitust korna; hwyrft hit of heofones lyfte...
anglo_saxon.translation: Hail is the whitest of grain; it is whirled from heaven.

practical_application: Chaotic disruption from outside forces.

name: Nauthiz

old_norwegian.original: Nauð gørver hneppa koste; nøktan kælr í froste.
old_norwegian.translation: Need makes a hard choice; the naked man freezes.

anglo_saxon.original: Nyd byþ nearu on breostan; weorþeþ hi þeah oft niþa bearnum...
anglo_saxon.translation: Trouble is oppressive to the heart; yet it often becomes a help.

practical_application: Resistance and the hard choices born of scarcity.

name: Isa

old_norwegian.original: Íss köllum brú breiða; blindan þarf at læiða.
old_norwegian.translation: Ice we call the broad bridge; the blind man must be led.

anglo_saxon.original: Is byþ ofereald, ungemetum slidor, glisniaþ glæshluttur...
anglo_saxon.translation: Ice is very cold and immeasurably slippery.

practical_application: Stasis, stillness, and careful navigation.

name: Jera

old_icelandic.original: Ár er gumna góði ok gott sumar ok algróinn akr.
old_icelandic.translation: Plenty is a boon to men and a thriving field.

anglo_saxon.original: Ger byþ gumena hiht, ðon God læteþ, halig heofones cyning...
anglo_saxon.translation: Summer is a joy to men when the earth brings forth fruits.

practical_application: Right action in the right season; reward of labor.

name: Eihwaz

anglo_saxon.original: Eoh byþ utan unsmþe treow, heard hruþan fæst, hyrde fyres...
anglo_saxon.translation: The yew is a tree with rough bark, a guardian of flame.

practical_application: Resilience and the bridge between worlds.

name: Perthro

anglo_saxon.original: Peorþ byþ symble plega ond hleahtor wlancum...
anglo_saxon.translation: Peorth is a source of recreation and amusement.

practical_application: Fate (Wyrd) and the unknown variables of chance.

name: Algiz

anglo_saxon.original: Eolh-secg eard hæfþ oftust on fenne, weaxeþ on wætere...
anglo_saxon.translation: The Elk-sedge makes a ghastly wound for those who touch it.

practical_application: Active deterrence and sanctuary protection.

name: Sowilo

old_icelandic.original: Sól er skýja skjöldr ok skínandi röðull ok ísa aldrtregi.
old_icelandic.translation: Sun is shield of the clouds and destroyer of ice.

anglo_saxon.original: Sigel semeth symble denum on hihte, ðon hi hine feriaþ...
anglo_saxon.translation: The sun is always a hope to seafarers.

practical_application: Victory, clarity, and breaking through stasis.

name: Tiwaz

old_icelandic.original: Týr er einhendr áss ok ulfs leifar ok hofa hilmir.
old_icelandic.translation: Tyr is a one-handed god and prince of temples.

anglo_saxon.original: Tir byþ tacna sum, healdeþ trywa wel wiþ æþelingas...
anglo_saxon.translation: Tiw is a guiding star; it keeps faith well with princes.

practical_application: Sacrifice for order and unwavering focus.

name: Berkano

old_norwegian.original: Bjarkan er laufgrønster líma; Loki bar flæðar tíma.
old_norwegian.translation: Birch has the greenest leaves; Loki was fortunate in his deceit.

anglo_saxon.original: Beorc byþ bletsung-leas, þeah hit beorhte scine...
anglo_saxon.translation: The birch bears no fruit; yet it has shining branches.

practical_application: Renewal, birth, and the emergence of potential.

name: Ehwaz

anglo_saxon.original: Eoh byþ for eorlum æþelinga wyn, hors hofum wlanc...
anglo_saxon.translation: The horse is a joy to princes in the presence of warriors.

practical_application: Partnership and the momentum of a shared bond.

name: Mannaz

old_icelandic.original: Maðr er moldar auki ok skipa skreytir ok sjávar svelgr.
old_icelandic.translation: Man is augmentation of the dust and adorner of ships.

anglo_saxon.original: Man byþ on myrgþe his magan leof: sceal ðeah anra gehwylc oðrum swican...
anglo_saxon.translation: The joyous man is dear; yet every man is doomed to fail his fellow.

practical_application: The human condition and the necessity of social support.

name: Laguz

old_norwegian.original: Lögr er, fællr ór fjalle foss; en gull ero hnoss.
old_norwegian.translation: A waterfall falls from a mountain-side; ornaments are made of gold.

anglo_saxon.original: Lagu byþ leodum langsum geþuht, gif hi sculun neþan on nacan tealtum...
anglo_saxon.translation: The ocean seems interminable to men on the unsteady bark.

practical_application: Intuition and the deep flow of the unready life.

name: Ingwaz

anglo_saxon.original: Ing wæs ærest mid East-Denum gesewen secgun...
anglo_saxon.translation: Ing was first seen by men among the East-Danes.

practical_application: Internalized growth and potential waiting to burst.

name: Dagaz

anglo_saxon.original: Dæg byþ Drihtnes sond, deore mannum, mære Metodes leoht...
anglo_saxon.translation: Day, the glorious light of the Creator, is sent by the Lord.

practical_application: Transformation and the radical shift from dark to light.

name: Othala

anglo_saxon.original: Eþel byþ oferleof æghwylcum men, gif he mot ðær rihtes...
anglo_saxon.translation: An estate is very dear to every man if he can enjoy whatever is right.

practical_application: Ancestral legacy and the heritage of one's home.
